skill.name: django-pro
skill.description: Advanced Django 5.x guidance for architecture, async views, Django REST Framework, Celery, Channels, testing, optimization, and deployment.
skill.summary: Build scalable Django applications with production-focused architecture, testing, security, and performance guidance.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported one external command pattern and four weak cryptographic algorithm patterns, but all were false positives in Markdown prose. The backticks at SKILL.md:26 mark a referenced file path, and the weak-crypto matches at SKILL.md:3, SKILL.md:39, SKILL.md:49, and SKILL.md:174 are ordinary descriptive text, not cryptographic code. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, executable script, or unsafe command behavior was found in the reviewed skill file.

content.value_statement: Django projects often need careful choices across architecture, ORM performance, async work, APIs, and deployment. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code focused Django guidance for building maintainable production systems.
actual_capabilities: Guides Django 5.x architecture decisions for reusable apps and service layers.
Helps design Django REST Framework APIs with authentication and permissions.
Suggests ORM optimization patterns using select_related, prefetch_related, annotations, and indexes.
Provides direction for async views, ASGI deployment, Django Channels, and Celery tasks.
Covers testing with pytest-django, DRF clients, factories, and coverage analysis.
Reviews deployment concerns such as static files, environment settings, containers, and CI/CD.
limitations: It provides guidance and examples, but it does not run or inspect a live Django server by itself.
It depends on the user supplying project context, models, settings, and performance symptoms.
It does not replace security review for authentication, payment, or production deployment changes.
The referenced implementation playbook is not included in the packaged file structure.

use_cases.title: Design a New Django Backend
use_cases.description: Plan app boundaries, settings structure, API layers, background jobs, and deployment choices before implementation starts.

use_cases.title: Improve Slow ORM Queries
use_cases.description: Review query patterns, N+1 risks, indexes, annotations, caching options, and test coverage for performance-sensitive views.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Production Release
use_cases.description: Check security middleware, environment configuration, static and media handling, worker setup, tests, and deployment workflow.

prompt_templates.title: Explain a Django Pattern
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain the best Django pattern for this feature. Include model, view, URL, template or API layer, and testing considerations.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Django Implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Django implementation for maintainability, security, ORM efficiency, and test coverage. Prioritize concrete issues and fixes.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize API Performance
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this DRF endpoint for query count, serializer cost, permissions, pagination, caching, and database indexes. Recommend measurable improvements.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Scalable Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a production Django architecture for this multi-tenant product. Cover app boundaries, data model, async work, Celery, Channels, observability, and deployment.

output_examples.input: I have a DRF list endpoint with slow responses and many repeated queries.
output: Identifies likely N+1 query sources in serializers and related fields.
Recommends select_related, prefetch_related, pagination, and targeted indexes.
Suggests tests that assert query counts and response behavior.

output_examples.input: I need a Django architecture for a SaaS product with background jobs.
output: Defines app boundaries for accounts, billing, notifications, and core domain logic.
Places long-running work in Celery tasks with idempotent retry behavior.
Covers settings separation, deployment workers, monitoring, and security checks.

output_examples.input: I want to add real-time notifications to an existing Django app.
output: Compares polling, Server-Sent Events, and Django Channels for the use case.
Outlines ASGI, channel layers, authentication, and permission handling.
Adds testing and deployment notes for WebSocket behavior.

best_practices: Provide models, serializers, views, settings, and expected traffic when asking for architecture or performance guidance.
Ask for tests and migration strategy whenever the answer changes data models or production behavior.
Validate recommendations against Django documentation, project constraints, and security requirements before deployment.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for broad Django advice without describing the project version, database, deployment target, and problem.
Do not apply ORM optimization suggestions without measuring query counts and response times.
Do not treat generated authentication, payment, or deployment code as production-ready without review.

faq.question: What Django versions does this skill target?
faq.answer: It focuses on Django 5.x while using patterns that often apply to recent supported Django releases.

faq.question: Can it help with Django REST Framework?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers DRF APIs, serializers, authentication, permissions, testing, and performance considerations.

faq.question: Can it optimize database queries?
faq.answer: Yes. It can suggest ORM improvements, indexes, annotations, prefetching, caching, and query-count tests.

faq.question: Does it support async Django work?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers async views, ASGI deployment, Django Channels, Celery, and related architecture choices.

faq.question: Is it only for new projects?
faq.answer: No. It can also review existing code, migration plans, deployment settings, and performance issues.

faq.question: Does it execute Django commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. The skill provides guidance and examples. Any command execution depends on the host AI tool and user approval.
